info.description: This is the API which allows to configure, start, stop backups and view reports
info.title: Cloudbackup API

tags.name: config
tags.description: Configuration of the server and backups it does

tags.name: backup
tags.description: Start, stop and see the status backups

tags.name: restore
tags.description: Start, stop and see the status restore operations

tags.name: report
tags.description: Reports about previous backups and restores

post.description: If a password field is specified with value "******" (number of asterisks does not matter as long as it is only asterisks) then the actual password will be copied from the current running configuration. This is because when you fetch the configuration (via GET) passwords do not get printed. Any entries from the old configuration which are skipped or left out will be replaced with default values.

get.description: Get the current active configuration. Password fields will have their values replaced with "**************" despite what the Swagger example shows below.

post.summary: Overwrite an existing backup configuration or create a new entry
post.description: Each individual backup entry needs to have a unique "name" entry. If a password field is specified with value "******" (number of asterisks does not matter as long as it is only asterisks) then the actual password will be copied from the current running configuration. This is because when you fetch the configuration (via GET) passwords do not get printed. Any entries from the "backup" section of old configuration which are skipped or left out will be replaced with default values.

get.summary: List backup jobs and their status
get.description: Get the list of configured backup jobs and their status. If state="running" then the uuid field will contain the unique id of the running backup; otherwise the field is an empty string.

post.description: Start a backup job now. The selected backup job must not be already running. If the backup job is successfully started then the uuid field will contain the unique id of the running backup; otherwise the field is an empty string.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: name of the backup to start

404.description: No backup job matches the provided name

post.description: Stop a running backup. The selected backup job must be already running. You need to specify only the name of the backup job and any matching running job will be stopped. Optionally you can also specify an uuid associated with the backup name and only if the running backup job matches both name and id will then be the backup job stopped.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: name of the backup to stop and optionally the id

post.summary: Perform a dry run for a backup job.
post.description: Performs a dry run backup job now. The purpose of this operation is to report back what files would be considered for backup, what would be  excluded and the exclusion rule which matched and also reports on  errors produced when trying to get file properties. The response is http2 Server Side Events and requires a client capable of consuming it. Response codes != 200 will be regular json while response code 200 will  be followed by http2 SSE. Each received event for the 200 status code  will be in the format "data: {json object}" except the last line at the  end of the input which will start with "data: Completed run". Unfortunatelly  Swagger doesn't allow proper documentation of SSE so the response object  definition is the actual {json object} to be expected after the "data: "  field.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: name of the backup to start

404.description: No backup job matches the provided name

post.description: Continuously watches a specific backup job in order to show file, directory and symlinks backup progress. This is a best effort operation meaning that events will get discarded and not sent to the client if either the server produces more events per second than it can handle or if the client can't receive quickly enough events produced by the server. The response is http2 Server Side Events and requires a client capable of consuming it. Response codes != 200 will be regular json while response code 200 will be followed by http2 SSE. Each received event for the 200 status code  will be in the format "data: {json object}" except the last line at the end of the input which will be one of "data: Backup job has finished" or "data: Backup job failed to start. Check sever logs for details" or "Backup job was cancelled while it was running". Unfortunatelly  Swagger doesn't allow proper documentation of SSE so the response object  definition is the actual {json object} to be expected after the "data: "  field.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: name of the backup to start

404.description: No backup job matches the provided name

name.description: Only ASCII characters are allowed for this property.
Name of the backup target. Commas are not allowed characters.

type.description: Backend type (object store) used for storing backups. Must be one of: aws_s3, gcp_storage, azure_blob

start_time.description: A start time of 0001-01-01T00:00:00Z is the equivallent of null time and should be ignored for jobs having "state"="stopped"

file_content_bytes_read.description: So far during this backup run this many bytes of file content were read in order to be backed up. This implies that the file was detected to not have been backed up or to have had a change which requires it to be backed up. If more than one backup targets are used then this counter will increment multiple times for a given files as the content is read multiple times and uploaded to each object store.

rate_1min.description: Average disk bandwidth per second consumed reading file content during the last 1 minute. This will roughly translate to network bandwidth consumed for uploading the said file content. This excludes any bandwidth used to read file or directory properties.

rate_5min.description: Average disk bandwidth per second consumed reading file content during the last 5 minutes. This will roughly translate to network bandwidth consumed for uploading the said file content. This excludes any bandwidth used to read file or directory properties.

rate_15min.description: Average disk bandwidth per second consumed reading file content during the last 15 minutes. This will roughly translate to network bandwidth consumed for uploading the said file content. This excludes any bandwidth used to read file or directory properties.

stats_counters.description: This object is present in replies only if the job "state" is not "stopped"

examined_files.description: How many files had their properties examined. Excluded files (via the "exclusions" section) do not increment this counter. This counter will not include non regular files like symlinks (or any other non regular types). The only time symlinks are taken into account is when dereference=true in the configuration file as then symlinks will be followed and treated as what their target is.

examined_directories.description: How many directories had their properties examined. Excluded directories (via the "exclusions" section) do not increment this counter.

examined_symlinks.description: How many symlinks had their properties examined. This counter is incremented when dereference=false in the configuration file

examined_unknown.description: How may non regular files (excluding symlinks and directories) where examined. Example of non regular files are sockets, device drivers (/dev/sda1), etc.

failed_to_examine.description: While examining the properties of files and directories this may errors were encountered. Please get the logs for messages with level ERROR and WARNING produced by context="backup.scan" in order to get additional details. Items for which this counter got incremented also had one of "examined_files" or "examined_directories" incremented. An increment of this counter  means that most likely the said file or directory will not get  backed up.

excluded.description: How many files or directories where excluded due to matching some exclusion rule provided by the user (in the configuration file).

uploaded_files.description: How many files were uploaded so far during the run of this backup job

uploaded_directories.description: How many directories had their properties uploaded  so far during the run of this backup job

uploaded_symlinks.description: How many symlinks had their properties uploaded  so far during the run of this backup job

failed_to_upload_files.description: How many files produced irrecoverable errors  during the upload so far during the run of this backup job. This  implies said files were not successfuly backed up. Causes can be lack of access to read file content or failure to  send the file to the remote object store. Check the log file for  details.

failed_to_upload_directories.description: How many directories produced irrecoverable errors  during the upload so far during the run of this backup job. This  implies said directories were not successfuly backed up. Generally, for directories this means that the directory's properties were not backed up but its files could potentially still be backed up successfully. Causes can be failure to  send the file to the remote object store. Check the log file for  details.

failed_to_upload_symlinks.description: How many symlinks produced irrecoverable errors  during the upload so far during the run of this backup job. This  implies said symlinks were not successfuly backed up.  Causes can be failure to  send the file to the remote object store. Check the log file for  details.

failed_to_upload_unknown.description: How many unordinary files produced irrecoverable errors  during the upload so far during the run of this backup job. This  implies said unordinary were not successfuly backed up. An attempt to backup an unordinary file will always lead to a failure to upload. Examples of unoridinary files are sockets, device files (/dev/zero), etc. Unoridinary files should be excluded from backup. Check the logs in order to find out which files were found.

updated_metadata_for_files.description: How many files were detected to have had a properties change but their content itself was not changed so a metadata only updated was done as part of this backup run.

updated_metadata_for_directories.description: How many directories were detected to have had a properties change so a metadata only updated was done as part of this backup run.

updated_metadata_for_symlinks.description: How many symlinks were detected to have had a properties change so a metadata only updated was done as part of this backup run.

failed_to_update_metadata_for_files.description: How many files which were detected to have a properties change only, produced irrecoverable errors  during the backup of the said change . Check the log file for  details.

failed_to_update_metadata_for_directorie.description: How many directories which were detected to have a properties change only, produced irrecoverable errors  during the backup of the said change . Check the log file for  details.

failed_to_update_metadata_for_symlinks.description: How many symlinks which were detected to have a properties change only, produced irrecoverable errors  during the backup of the said change . Check the log file for  details.

stats_text.description: This object is present in replies only if the job "state" is not "stopped"

current_directory.description: The current directory being processed by the backup job which is in progress. If the field is empty then no directory is currently processed or processing has completed

current_file.description: The current file being processed by the backup job which is in progress. If the field is empty then no file is currently processed or processing has completed

name.description: Name of the file or directory being reported

type.description: Unknown is reported when the file was excluded by an exclusion rule as then its properties aren't fetched so it can't be determined if it was a file or directory. Unknown is also reported when an error was encountered when trying to get the properties so again it can't be established if it is a file or a directory. The "dryrun" function does not report separately symlinks, meaning they will be reported as of type "file".

excluded.description: If this file or directory matched an exclusion rule. If excluded then it will not be considered for a backup

exclusion_expr.description: The expression which matched the file or folder and caused it to be excluded from being considered for backup. This expression will correspond to one of the expressions mentioned in the configuration file. If $excluded==false then this will be an empty string

error.description: Empty string if no error was encountered and otherwise the error message produced when trying to get the file or directory properties

sequence.description: Incremented for each item being backed up. This is used in order to determine if messages were droppe due to the communication channel between the http server and the client being full. For each object at least one message will be sent and given size and other details it can be that many messages will be sent for one particular object. A jump of more than one for $sequence means that all the messages for one or more objects were lost. If at least one message is received by a client for a particular object then its impossible to tell if some of the messages for that particular sequence number were lost (the $percent_done field may offer additional clues in some cases).

name.description: Name of the file, symlink or directory being reported.

percent_done.description: For a given object, shows upload progress. For directories and symlinks the only two possible values are 0 and 100. In the case of directories, percent done 100 just means that the details about the directory (owner, group, etc) were backed up but this excludes actual files, directories and symlinks contained within said directory as those will each be separately reported. This also leads to the situation of getting a report of 100% percent done for a directory and then the next message being that it failed to examine the directory because the contents of the directory itself could not be enumerated.

rate.description: Upload speed in bytes per second for a given file. For directories and symlinks this will always have a values of 0.

type.description: Object currently being backed up: on of file, dir, symlink, unknown. Unknown is reported when an error was encountered while trying to get the properties so it can't be established if it is a file, directory or symlink. Another case for unknown is when the object is a non standard file (socket, unix pipe, etc).

store_name.description: Object store name (as defined in the configuration file) to which the files/directories and symlinks are being backed up to.

store_type.description: Object store type to which the files/directories and symlinks are being backed up to.

operation_type.description: One of "excluded", "examine", "upload" or "metadata" depicting if the message represents an examination of an object in order to determine if a backup is needed, content upload and metadata upload/update. The "excluded" value is reported only for excluded from backup objects(those are items which are located within the paths selected for backup but for which a configuration file entry has been set to purposely exclude them from the backup). The "examine" value is reported only when an error was produced during the examination step as otherwise a message won't be produced if this step is successful. The "upload" value is reported only for file content upload. The "metadata" value is reported when a properties change was detected for an already known (to be previously backed up) object or when a directory or symlink is backed up (as only properties and not actual content is uploaded for those).

error.description: Empty string if no error was encountered and otherwise the error message produced. An error means that most likely the object in question was not backed up successfully.

name.description: The name corresponds to the target name in the config file backup job definition

rate_1min.description: Average disk bandwidth per second consumed reading file content during the last 1 minute. This will roughly translate to network bandwidth consumed for uploading the said file content to this particular object store. This excludes any bandwidth used to read file or directory properties.

rate_5min.description: Average disk bandwidth per second consumed reading file content during the last 5 minutes. This will roughly translate to network bandwidth consumed for uploading the said file content to this particular object store. This excludes any bandwidth used to read file or directory properties.

rate_15min.description: Average disk bandwidth per second consumed reading file content during the last 15 minutes. This will roughly translate to network bandwidth consumed for uploading the said file content to this particular object store. This excludes any bandwidth used to read file or directory properties.
